methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $190M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Kneron is a Series B semiconductor chip company founded by Albert Liu and Frank Chang that has raised $190M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor firms, including same-stage peers such as Positron AI and adjacent-stage players including Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and DEEPX.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $190M total raised proxy, yielding $361M. Peer-median anchoring across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $1.0B implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied because Kneron's revenue is not on file; the public semiconductor median trades at 22.3x sales across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD, but lacks the necessary target revenue input.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $307M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $680M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Kneron tracks closer to early-stage, higher-multiple entrants like Etched ($5.0B) or more mature Series B/C cohorts. Key unknowns include revenue scale, customer concentration, and path to productization in a capital-intensive sector. The median estimate of $680M serves as the central case pending further financial disclosure.
